title: Delete ('D')
text: Delete ('D') Delete a record. Description For an SQL-like DBMS, the connector must use the uiflst structure. This identifies exactly one record by either the primary key or the PRA, and optionally a U_VERSION field. For an ISAM-like or record-level DBMSs, a Fetch mode 2 has prepared locking and positioning. The positioned record is the record to be deleted. From Uniface Preconditions realized by Uniface—The table for which the Delete is requested is open. Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->TMEnabled Indicates whether the connector is TM-controlled. uctrl->udrv Points to the connector workspace if a workspace was requested in the Info call. uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path. uctrl->ufldlst The field list which describes all the fields in the table. uctrl->uiflst Identifies the record to be deleted (SQL-based DBMSs only). uctrl->uilist Points to the index list. uctrl->sqlbuf Points to the SQL buffer. To Uniface Table 2. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Exactly one record was successfully deleted. -3 Exceptional error. -9 I/O server logon error. -16 DBMS reported network error. If the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr . If more than one record or zero records are deleted, the connector must return an error. Extra information When the row to delete contains segmented fields (that is, there are entries in the field list for which the bitwise AND of ufbits and the value 8 are non-zero), the connector must also delete the segmented fields. TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors TM-controlled DBMS connectors are not allowed to begin, commit, or rollback transactions. For more information, see the "Transactions" topic. Related Links uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table. Transactions Transactions play a particular role for stateful components, because these components usually remain active for long periods of time while passing through various states. In principle, each state requires that resources be allocated to it. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
